channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Our Ocean Conference: After Mombasa – Will Africa and the World Make Ocean Promises Real?
item.description: « James Alix Michel warns that without real finance and precaution, ocean pledges risk remaining only on paper. » Now that the lights have dimmed in Mombasa and the delegations have gone home, a simple but necessary question remains: did the first Our Ocean Conference on African soil truly move the world from promises to […]

item.title: The UN Human Rights Office Moves Out of the Palais Wilson
item.description: GENEVA -- Since 1998, the United Nations Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights, or OHCHR, has been based in the historic Palais Wilson in this Swiss city, formerly in the building that first housed the League of Nations, from 1920 to 1936. But this summer, because of the agency’s long-term plans to relocate, sped up by UN systemwide budget reforms, the high commissioner, Volker Turk, and his human rights staff are moving across town from the rarefied waterfront setting to a new site, called Building H, on the UN Ariana campus of the Palais des Nations. In this original photo essay, taken in Geneva in early June, we give you a glimpse of the physical features of the Palais Wilson – which overlooks Lake Geneva -- and the relocation to Building H, which offers views of the city

item.title: Afghanistan in crisis: Drought, malnutrition and a worsening humanitarian situation
item.description: Imagine being one of a family of nine and sitting down to a meal of potato peelings and other scraps, boiled up into a soup. This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable families, forced on them by climate change and drought, widespread malnutrition and increasing restrictions on women, since the Taliban overran Kabul in 2021.

item.title: World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk
item.description: Four decades after AIDS first emerged as a global crisis, world leaders, advocates and community representatives gathered at UN Headquarters on Monday issued a stark warning: progress against HIV is slowing just as financial pressures and shrinking support threaten to reverse decades of gains.

item.title: ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears
item.description: Syria has emerged after more than a decade of crisis with “clear signs of progress”, but acute needs remain and efforts must boost support so the country can “move from survival towards recovery”, UN officials told the Security Council on Monday.

item.title: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks
item.description: A decline in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Monday.

item.title: AI is getting women wrong as gender bias persists, data reveals
item.description: As artificial intelligence reshapes how people work, communicate and access information, UN Women warned on Monday that the technology is reproducing old gender stereotypes, which amplify online abuse and leave women out of the decisions that will define the digital future.

item.title: World News in Brief: Pope Leo urges action on hunger, humanitarian strain deepens in Gaza, families return to Lebanon
item.description: Pope Leo called on the international community to renew its commitment to tackling hunger and malnutrition, describing access to adequate food as a “fundamental human right” during a visit to the World Food Programme (WFP) headquarters in Rome on Monday.

item.title: Sudan: Drone attacks endanger civilians and shut down critical services in El Obeid
item.description: Escalating violence in and around the Sudanese city of El Obeid is putting civilians at increased risk and disrupting essential services, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Monday.

item.title: Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine
item.description: The Security Council is holding an open meeting on Ukraine on Monday afternoon, with warnings of a “dangerous cycle of escalation” amid drone and missile attacks in briefings by Mohamed Khaled Khiari, Assistant Secretary-General at the Departments of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs and Peace Operations, and Edem Wosornu, crisis response director at the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, OCHA.

item.title: Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable
item.description: As global confirmed Ebola cases reach 1,000, nearly three million children and adolescents are at risk in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while efforts increase to treat prisoners near the epicentre of the current outbreak, UN agencies warned on Monday.

item.title: Dwindling Humanitarian Aid Devastates the Rohingyas in the World’s Largest Refugee Camp
item.description: Nearly nine years after the violent persecution of the Rohingya minority population in Myanmar and the following mass exodus of refugees, over 1.2 million Rohingya currently reside in neighbouring Bangladesh, where they face immense challenges. With the United Nations (UN) recording significant shortfalls in global humanitarian funding, alongside Bangladesh’s diminishing ability to support these populations, […]

item.title: Global South Leaders Redesigning International Cooperation
item.description: The fallout from the sudden collapse of the old system of financing international cooperation has been disastrous, unleashing a wave of harm and leaving the world more vulnerable to shocks and less able to respond to them. The wreckage is plain to see. The issue is what to do next. Calling attention to the damage […]

item.title: In 2025, Government Forces were the Greatest Perpetrators of Violence Against Children in Armed Conflicts
item.description: A record number of children were subject to grave violations by parties to armed conflicts, the highest since the UN mandate for children and armed conflict (CAAC) was established in 1996. In the Secretary-General’s annual report, UN-verified sources confirmed 35,558 violations committed against children during armed conflicts. This is the fourth year in a row […]

item.title: A UN Secretary-General who Defied the US– and Suffered a Backlash
item.description: When Egypt’s onetime Foreign Minister Boutros Boutros-Ghali was running for the post of U.N. Secretary-General in late 1991, he had to contend with the rival candidature of Bernard Chidzero, then foreign minister of Zimbabwe. As the campaign began to intensify, Boutros-Ghali recounted a brief encounter with Chidzero, a longstanding friend, at a conference in Africa, […]
